newsSinceJun6: May 2026 monthly revenue published on KYEC's IR page (Taiwan 10th-of-month disclosure window): NT$3,776.9M, +36.7% YoY and a fresh monthly record, sequentially above April (NT$3,743.0M, +34.5% YoY) and March (NT$3,597.9M, +36.1% YoY). Confirms utilization/test-intensity thesis on top of recent context: 2026 capex raised from NT$39.4B to NT$50B, Singapore CP/FT/BI/SLT plant commenced operations May 22, and KYEC's role as primary final tester for Nvidia B300/B200/H200. Stock barely reacted: +0.87% today, +8.8% YTD.

sources.label: KYEC IR - Monthly revenue (May 2026 NT$3.78B, +36.7% YoY)
DigiTimes - KYEC record 1Q26 revenue, capex raised to NT$50B
TrendForce - ASE/PTI/KYEC capex may hit NT$370B as AI drives record OSAT investment
Singapore EDB - KYEC inaugurates Singapore facility (May 22, 2026)
Dan Nystedt - KYEC is primary tester for Nvidia B300/B200/H200

verdicts.rankRationale: Genuinely new evidence since June 6: record May revenue (+36.7% YoY, third straight ~35%+ month) directly validates the utilization/test-intensity leg of the thesis, while the stock has not rerated (+0.87% today, +8.8% YTD vs an 11x larger capex commitment). Rank 22 understates a bottleneck node where every Nvidia B300/B200/H200 passes through its testers; the move respects yesterday's 'up' because today's print is new, not a re-argument.
verdicts.bull: Primary final-test gateway for Nvidia's flagship AI silicon with record May revenue, capex raised 26% to NT$50B, and a freshly opened Singapore plant - utilization is confirmed accelerating while the stock trades far below the ~NT$354 consensus target, leaving uncaptured rerating.
verdicts.neutral: Test services are real but lower-margin and cyclical; +35% YoY revenue growth is already partly anticipated by 14 buy ratings, so the name is known to the sell side even if price hasn't followed yet.
verdicts.bear: OSAT test capacity can be replicated faster than fab or HBM capacity; if AI test-time intensity plateaus or Nvidia diversifies test partners, the NT$50B capex becomes a margin headwind into any 2027 digestion phase.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: June/Q2 monthly revenue prints sustaining ~35%+ YoY plus B300 ramp test volumes, with Singapore fab contribution starting 2H26.
verdicts.primaryRisk: AI test outsourcing concentration risk - a shift of Nvidia/AMD final-test allocation or an OSAT capex glut compresses pricing just as record capacity comes online.
